Automation tools

Databases and backend frameworks

Daytona is the open-source dev environment manager that supercharges your productivity. Think of us as the Codespaces alternative that puts you in control, on any machine. No vendor lock-in here. Spin up fully-configured dev environments in seconds, perfectly tailored to your project's needs. No more wasting hours on setup and configuration. We're backed by an all-star group of founders from companies like Postman, Netlify, Supabase, StackOverflow, Honeycomb, Puppet, Sentry, Warp, and others. [...] 5.0

Based on 10 reviews

Review Daytona?

Reviewers mostly praise Daytona for fast, reliable sandboxed environments, especially for AI and agent workloads. Users say setup is easy, SDKs fit existing Python and TypeScript workflows, and startup speed, stateful execution, Docker support, and multi-region operation help reduce latency and improve responsiveness. Feedback from the makers of Crossnode and Grass adds technical detail: they use Daytona for secure, isolated, persistent Linux containers and remote workspaces, saying it saved them from building and managing that infrastructure themselves.

SocialLinkedInX

Founder Reviews(8) [...] Crossnode

used Daytona to build Crossnode

•4 reviews

To run a multi-tenant platform safely, we needed enterprise-grade sandboxing. Daytona gives us secure, isolated, and stateful Linux environments instantly. If an agent needs to write a Python script, install a package, or run a bash command on behalf of a client, Daytona ensures it happens in a completely secure container without risking our core infrastructure. It let us skip building complex Docker management and focus entirely on the AI orchestration layer.

We have secrets spread across a Next.js frontend, a Python backend, multiple worker processes, and dev/staging/production environments. Doppler keeps all of that in sync without .env files scattered everywhere. For a product that connects to a dozen different APIs (Slack, Notion, GitHub, CRM, etc.), secret management is not optional.

results.title: Modal Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
•8 reviews

Modals VM's power our AI agent environments, allowing them to view pull requests, inspect files, and even execute code! Thanks to the Modal team for prioritizing our feature requests and building a GREAT headless compute environment.

#### What's great

headless compute environment (1)

Report

 16 views

Viktor for Microsoft Teams

used Modal to build Viktor for Microsoft Teams

•9 reviews [...] 5.0

Based on 54 reviews

Review Modal?

Reviewers are uniformly positive, though the feedback comes almost entirely from founders rather than end users. The clearest pattern is ease: teams say Modal makes serious compute, GPU jobs, and sandboxes feel simple to spin up, often from Python, without managing Kubernetes, Dockerfiles, or cloud infrastructure. Makers of Fathom, Kodezi, and viktor.com describe fast startup times, clean workflows, reliable scaling to zero, and a strong developer experience for prototyping and production workloads.

+52

Summarized with AI

Pros

Cons

Reviews

All Reviews

Most Informative

Subscribe

Pros

inference at scale(4)

training pipelines(3)

cold start management(2)

fine-tuning models(2)

Python hosting(2)

scalable GPU serving(2)

SocialLinkedIn [...] used Modal to build Viktor for Microsoft Teams

•9 reviews

Viktor runs its agents server-side, 24/7, which means spinning up isolated sandboxes for thousands of teams at once and scaling back to zero the moment they go idle. We looked at rolling our own on Kubernetes and at the usual serverless suspects. Modal was the only one where a Python engineer could define the whole sandbox, schedule, and scaling story in code and ship it the same afternoon. It is a big part of why a small team can run this much compute without standing up a platform team to babysit it.

#### Alternatives Considered

results.title: Boxes.dev: Run Claude Code and Codex in your own cloud environment | Product Hunt
results.content:
Nick Bushak
Boxes.dev

Boxes.dev

Hi Product Hunt! It's great to be back with a new launch. Fond memories of launching my last company, Gem, here way back in 2017!   
  
We’re Nick and Drew, and we’re building boxes.dev – the first cloud-only agentic dev environment (ADE) that gives every Codex and Claude Code agent its own cloud computer.  
  
We spent the last year coding almost exclusively with Codex / Claude Code, but got tired of leaving our laptops cracked open and dealing with clunky git worktrees. Developing on localhost was holding us back, so we decided to build the cloud-based ADE that we wished existed.  
  
We’re obviously biased, but we’ve been building boxes.dev with boxes.dev for months and it’s been a gamechanger – it’s hard to imagine going back.   
  
Key features: [...] Ian Xu

This looks great. I spend a lot of time running coding agents so giving each one its own machine makes a lot of sense. Can you run several agents in parallel on the same repo?

Nick Bushak
Boxes.dev

Boxes.dev

@ianhxu Yes! This was the primary motivation for us -- you can have multiple threads/agents running in parallel, each with their own totally independent machine. No more running out of resources locally or having to juggle git worktrees. Since we have a template/main box that you have full control of, there's also no setup required per-thread/per-machine, each thread's VM starts from a perfect snapshot of the template box.

Samyak Sanklecha

One thing that seems under-discussed with cloud coding environments is continuity. [...] Super easy setup (our agent scans your local dev setup and ports it to the cloud)

Full-featured desktop, CLI, and mobile app (not just handoffs or remote control)

Uses your Claude Code / Codex subscription (native harnesses & familiar UX)

Your coding agents can run/test your full app end-to-end in isolation

Scheduled automations & Slack integration

Optimized for parallel agent workflows

All new users get 10 free box-hours to test it out at boxes.dev. Hope you’ll give it a try – would love any and all feedback!

Patrick Kavanagh
Atlantic Money

Atlantic Money

@nbushak Looks great! Can the agents spin up playwright for tests?

Nick Bushak
Boxes.dev

Boxes.dev
